devanagari: भगवतः परमधामगमनम् । श्रीशुक उवाच । अथ तत्रागमद् ब्रह्मा भवान्या च समं भवः । महेंद्रप्रमुखा देवा मुनयः सप्रजेश्वराः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: bhagavataḥ paramadhāmagamanam śrīśuka uvāca atha tatrāgamad brahmā bhavānyā ca samaṃ bhavaḥ maheṃdrapramukhā devā munayaḥ saprajeśvarāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

devanagari: पितरः सिद्धगंधर्वा विद्याधरमहोरगाः । चारणा यक्षरक्षांसि किन्नराप्सरसो द्विजाः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: pitaraḥ siddhagaṃdharvā vidyādharamahoragāḥ cāraṇā yakṣarakṣāṃsi kinnarāpsaraso dvijāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second single], [aorist active second single]

devanagari: द्रष्टुकामा भगवतो निर्याणं परमोत्सुकाः । गायंतश्च गृणंतश्च शौरेः कर्माणि जन्म च ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: draṣṭukāmā bhagavato niryāṇaṃ paramotsukāḥ gāyaṃtaśca gṛṇaṃtaśca śaureḥ karmāṇi janma ca

devanagari: ववृषुः पुष्पवर्षाणि विमानावलिभिर्नभः । कुर्वंतः सङ्कुलं राजन् भक्त्या परमया युताः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: vavṛṣuḥ puṣpavarṣāṇi vimānāvalibhirnabhaḥ kurvaṃtaḥ saṅkulaṃ rājan bhaktyā paramayā yutāḥ

devanagari: भगवान् पितामहं वीक्ष्य विभूतीरात्मनो विभुः । संयोज्यात्मनि चात्मानं पद्मनेत्रे न्यमीलयत् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: bhagavān pitāmahaṃ vīkṣya vibhūtīrātmano vibhuḥ saṃyojyātmani cātmānaṃ padmanetre nyamīlayat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]
[nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: Beholding god Brahmā (the grand-sire of theuniverse) and other celestials who were his own glorious manifestations, that all-pervading Lord concentrated his mind on the Supreme Self and closed his lotus eyes in samādhi (meditation) with a view to avoid the expected requests of the gods to visit their respective regions.

devanagari: लोकाभिरामां स्वतनुं धारणा ध्यान मङ्गलम् । योगधारणयाऽग्नेय्या दग्ध्वा धामाविशत्स्वकम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: lokābhirāmāṃ svatanuṃ dhāraṇā dhyāna maṅgalam yogadhāraṇayā
translation: By the technical Yogic process called Āgneyī whereby the Yogi concentrates his mind on fire and burns down his body, the Lord did not consume with fire his world-enchanting form which was very auspicious for concentration and meditation (and was the support of the world) but entered with his body his own realm[1]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Contrast Mahābhārata Mausala 7.31.
tataḥ śarīre rāmasya vāsudeuasya co'bhayoḥ /
anviṣya dāhayāmāsa puruṣair āptakāribhiḥ //

devanagari: दिवि दुंदुभयो नेदुः पेतुः सुमनसश्च खात् । सत्यं धर्मो धृतिर्भूमेः कीर्तिः श्रीश्चानु तं ययुः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: divi duṃdubhayo neduḥ petuḥ sumanasaśca khāt satyaṃ dharmo dhṛtirbhūmeḥ kīrtiḥ śrīścānu taṃ yayuḥ
translation: Kettledrums were sounded in the heavens; flowers were showered down from the sky. And Truth, Righteousness, Fortitude, Glory, and Prosperity quitted the earth and followed him (to Vaikuṇṭha).

devanagari: देवादयो ब्रह्ममुख्या न विशंतं स्वधामनि । अविज्ञातगतिं कृष्णं ददृशुश्चातिविस्मिताः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: devādayo brahmamukhyā na viśaṃtaṃ svadhāmani avijñātagatiṃ kṛṣṇaṃ dadṛśuścātivismitāḥ
translation: Gods and others (who assembled there), of whom god Brahmā was prominent, were not able to behold (or even cognise) Lord Kṛṣṇa of inscrutable ways, while he was entering his own realm (Vaikuṇṭha) and they were left dazed in utter astonishment.

devanagari: सौदामन्या यथाऽकाशे यांत्या हित्वाभ्रमण्डलम् । गतिर्न लक्ष्यते मर्त्यैः तथा कृष्णस्य दैवतैः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: saudāmanyā yathā gatirna lakṣyate martyaiḥ tathā kṛṣṇasya daivataiḥ
translation: The passing away of Lord Kṛṣṇa could not be perceived by gods, just as the trail of lightening in the sky after its passing across the mass of clouds cannot be seen by (mortal) human beings.

devanagari: ब्रह्मरुद्रादयस्ते तु दृष्ट्वा योगगतिं हरेः । विस्मितास्तां प्रशंसंतः स्वं स्वं लोकं ययुस्तदा ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: brahmarudrādayaste tu dṛṣṭvā yogagatiṃ hareḥ vismitāstāṃ praśaṃsaṃtaḥ svaṃ svaṃ lokaṃ yayustadā
translation: Witnessing the (incomprehensible) Yogic way of Hari’s disappearance, gods beaded by Brahmā, Rudra and others were wonder-struck and glorifying it, they returned to their respective regions.

devanagari: राजन् परस्य तनुभृज्जननाप्ययेहा । मायाविडम्बनमवेहि यथा नटस्य । सृष्ट्वात्मनेदमनुविश्य विहृत्य चांते । संहृत्य चात्ममहिनोपरतः स आस्ते ॥ ...
english_devnagari: rājan parasya tanubhṛjjananāpyayehā māyāviḍambanamavehi yathā naṭasya sṛṣṭvātmanedamanuviśya vihṛtya cāṃte saṃhṛtya cātmamahinoparataḥ sa āste

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: O King, please note that the incarnation, disappearance and the (sportive) activities among human beings (Yādavas) of the transcendental Lord, are nothing but histrionics staged by dint of his Māyā Potency, like those of a theatrical performer. Having created this universe by his great Will Power (without any outside material or agency), and having entered it as Antaryāmin (Inner Controller) and sported (into it), he withdraws it within himself in the end, and abides established in his own (glorious) state.

devanagari: मर्त्येन यो गुरुसुतं यमलोकनीतं । त्वां चानयच्छरणदः परमास्त्रदग्धम् । जिग्येंऽतकांतकमपीशमसावनीशः । किं स्वावने स्वरनयन् मृगयुं सदेहम् ॥...
english_devnagari: martyena yo gurusutaṃ yamalokanītaṃ tvāṃ cānayaccharaṇadaḥ paramāstradagdham jigyeṃ kiṃ svāvane svaranayan mṛgayuṃ sadeham

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ nī class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ nī class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ svṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ svṛ class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ svar class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ svar class 1 verb]

translation: Was that Lord incapable of protecting his own person who with(i.e. while he was in)his mortal body brought back, to his teacher Sāndīpani, his son who had been snatched away to the region of Death (Yama), who restored you to life while you were (practically) burnt by a great missile (Brahmāstra discharged by Aśvatthāman) and who vanquished Rudra, the destroyer of Yama and transported (his assassin) the hunter bodily to the celestial world?

devanagari: तथाप्यशेषस्थितिसम्भवाप्ययेषु । अनन्यहेतुर्यदशेषशक्तिधृक् । नैच्छत् प्रणेतुं वपुरत्र शेषितं । मर्त्येन किं स्वस्थगतिं प्रदर्शयन् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: tathāpyaśeṣasthitisambhavāpyayeṣu ananyaheturyadaśeṣaśaktidhṛk naicchat praṇetuṃ vapuratra śeṣitaṃ martyena kiṃ svasthagatiṃ pradarśayan

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ śiṣ ], [accusative single from √ śiṣ ]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Though he alone was the sole cause of the subsistence) evolution and destruction of the entire universe as he was the wholesole controller of all powers, he did not wish to leave his person behind in this mortal worlds as there was no propriety in retaining a mortal body in this world and wanted to demonstrate the glorious state of those who are established in the Self. (Had he left his body in this world, the Yogins would have preferred to stay on in this world.

devanagari: य एतां प्रातरुत्थाय कृष्णस्य पदवीं पराम् । प्रयतः कीर्तयेद् भक्त्या तामेवाप्नोत्यनुत्तमाम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: ya etāṃ prātarutthāya kṛṣṇasya padavīṃ parām prayataḥ kīrtayed bhaktyā tāmevāpnotyanuttamām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ pre class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ pre class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ pre class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ pre class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ pre class 2 verb]

translation: He who gets up early in the morning and devoutly glorifies the passage to the Supreme State by Lord Kṛṣṇa, will reach that highest stage to which none is superior.

devanagari: दारुको द्वारकामेत्य वसुदेवोग्रसेनयोः । पतित्वा चरणावस्रैः न्यषिञ्चत् कृष्णविच्युतः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: dāruko dvārakāmetya vasudevograsenayoḥ patitvā caraṇāvasraiḥ nyaṣiñcat kṛṣṇavicyutaḥ
translation: Dāruka ( the charioteer of Kṛṣṇa), bereaved of Kṛṣṇa arrived at Dvārakā. He fell at the feet of Vasudeva and Ugrasena and bathed them with tears profusely.

devanagari: कथयामास निधनं वृष्णीनां कृत्स्नशो नृप । तच्छ्रुत्वोद्विग्नहृदया जनाः शोकविर्मूर्च्छिताः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: kathayāmāsa nidhanaṃ vṛṣṇīnāṃ kṛtsnaśo nṛpa tacchrutvodvignahṛdayā janāḥ śokavirmūrcchitāḥ
translation: He reported to them, O King, of the total destruction of the Yādava clans. Hearing that heart-rending tale, people (of Dvārakā) were over-whelmed with grief and fainted.

devanagari: तत्र स्म त्वरिता जग्मुः कृष्णविश्लेषविह्वलाः । व्यसवः शेरते यत्र ज्ञातयो घ्नंत आननम् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: tatra sma tvaritā jagmuḥ kṛṣṇaviśleṣavihvalāḥ vyasavaḥ śerate yatra jñātayo ghnaṃta ānanam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ tvar class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ tvar class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ tvar ], [vocative plural from √ tvar ]

[nominative plural from √ tvar class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ tvar class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ tvar class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ tvar ], [vocative plural from √ tvar ], [accusative plural from √ tvar ]
[nominative plural from √ han class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: Deeply agitated at their bereavement from Kṛṣṇa and beating their faces and heads (with uncontrollable grief), they hastily rushed to the spot where their kinsmen lay lifeless.

devanagari: देवकी रोहिणी चैव वसुदेवस्तथा सुतौ । कृष्णरामावपश्यंतः शोकार्ता विजहुः स्मृतिम् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: devakī rohiṇī caiva vasudevastathā sutau kṛṣṇarāmāvapaśyaṃtaḥ śokārtā vijahuḥ smṛtim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ su class 5 verb], [vocative dual from √ su class 5 verb], [accusative dual from √ su class 5 verb]
[nominative dual from √ sū class 6 verb], [vocative dual from √ sū class 6 verb], [accusative dual from √ sū class 6 verb]
[nominative dual from √ sū class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ sū class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ sū class 2 verb]

translation: When Devakī, Ṛohiṇī as well as Vasudeva were unable to see their sons Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa, they were so afflicted with grief that they lost their consciousness.

devanagari: प्राणांश्च विजहुस्तत्र भगवद् विरहातुराः । उपगुह्य पतींस्तात चितां आरुरुहुः स्त्रियः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: prāṇāṃśca vijahustatra bhagavad virahāturāḥ upaguhya patīṃstāta citāṃ āruruhuḥ striyaḥ
translation: They were so intensly stricken with grief at their separation from Lord Kṛṣṇa that they gave up their life then and there[2]. The Yādava woman embraced their husbands and ascended the funeral pyres (burnt themselves in the fire).
purport:
Footnote [2]:
The Mahābhārata differs. After Arjuna’s arrival at Dvārakā, he held consultations with Vasudeva about taking away Yādava men and women with their kit. Vasudeva cast off his ghost by the Yogic process, and his four chief queens immolated themselves in the funeral pyre of Vasudeva—Mausala 7.15-25.

devanagari: रामपत्न्यश्च तद्देहं उपगुह्याग्निमाविशन् । वसुदेवपत्‍न्यस्तद्‍गात्रं प्रद्युम्नादीन् हरेः स्नुषाः । कृष्णपत्न्योऽविशन्नग्निं रुक्मिण्य...
english_devnagari: rāmapatnyaśca taddehaṃ upaguhyāgnimāviśan vasudevapat‍nyastad‍gātraṃ pradyumnādīn hareḥ snuṣāḥ kṛṣṇapatnyo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ ad ], [vocative plural from √ ad ]
[nominative plural from √ ad ], [vocative plural from √ ad ], [accusative plural from √ ad ]

translation: The wives of Balarāma as well clasped his body and entered fire. Queens of Vasudeva embraced his body and the daughters-in-law of Kṛṣṇa clasped each the bodies of their respective husbands like Pradyumna and others, and consorts of Kṛṣṇa like Ṛukmiṇī and others with their hearts fixed on him entered the fire.[3]
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Mahābhārata slates that only Rukmiṇī, Gāndhārī, Śaibyā, Haimayati and Jāmbavatī immolated themselves in fire, while Satyabhāmā and other dear wives of Kṛṣṇa went to the forest for performing penance.—Mausala 773-74..

devanagari: अर्जुनः प्रेयसः सख्युः कृष्णस्य विरहातुरः । आत्मानं सांत्वयामास कृष्णगीतैः सदुक्तिभिः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: arjunaḥ preyasaḥ sakhyuḥ kṛṣṇasya virahāturaḥ ātmānaṃ sāṃtvayāmāsa kṛṣṇagītaiḥ saduktibhiḥ
translation: Deeply afflicted with the grief of separation from his dearest friend Kṛṣṇa, Arjuna consoled himself by pondering over the words of spiritual wisdom addressed to him(in the Bhagavad Gītā and the Anu Gītā) by Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: बंधूनां नष्टगोत्राणां अर्जुनः सांपरायिकम् । हतानां कारयामास यथावद् अनुपूर्वशः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: baṃdhūnāṃ naṣṭagotrāṇāṃ arjunaḥ sāṃparāyikam hatānāṃ kārayāmāsa yathāvad anupūrvaśaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ naś class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ naś class 4 verb]
[vocative single from √ naś class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ naś class 4 verb]
[genitive plural from √ han class 1 verb], [genitive plural from √ han class 2 verb]
[genitive plural from √ han class 1 verb], [genitive plural from √ han class 2 verb]
[genitive plural from √ han class 1 verb], [genitive plural from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: In the order of seniority of his relatives who were killed in the fight and had their races cut off, Arjuna arranged for the proper performance of the obsequies (for their welfare in the next world.)

devanagari: द्वारकां हरिणा त्यक्तां समुद्रोऽप्लावयत् क्षणात् । वर्जयित्वा महाराज श्रीमद् भगवदालयम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: dvārakāṃ hariṇā tyaktāṃ samudro varjayitvā mahārāja śrīmad bhagavadālayam
translation: The sea immediately inundated the city of Dvārakā forsaken by Hari, with the exception of the palace of the Lord, O great king.

devanagari: नित्यं सन्निहितस्तत्र भगवान् मधुसूदनः । स्मृत्याशेषाशुभहरं सर्वमङ्गलमङ्गलम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: nityaṃ sannihitastatra bhagavān madhusūdanaḥ smṛtyāśeṣāśubhaharaṃ sarvamaṅgalamaṅgalam
translation: It is the auspicious-most of the auspicious and by its very remembrance it removes all evils (including sins), for the glorious Lord Kṛṣṇa, the slayer of demon Madhu, always abides there.

devanagari: स्त्रीबालवृद्धानादाय हतशेषान् धनञ्जयः । इंद्रप्रस्थं समावेश्य वज्रं तत्राभ्यषेचयत् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: strībālavṛddhānādāya hataśeṣān dhanañjayaḥ iṃdraprasthaṃ samāveśya vajraṃ tatrābhyaṣecayat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Arjuna escorted women, children and old men, the survivors of the massacre and rehabilitated them at Indra- Prastha (the previous capital of Pāṇḍavas) and coronated Vajra (the son of Aniruddha, scion of the royal Yādava family) there, as their king.

devanagari: श्रुत्वा सुहृद् वधं राजन् अर्जुनात्ते पितामहाः । त्वां तु वंशधरं कृत्वा जग्मुः सर्वे महापथम् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrutvā suhṛd vadhaṃ rājan arjunātte pitāmahāḥ tvāṃ tu vaṃśadharaṃ kṛtvā jagmuḥ sarve mahāpatham

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: Having heard the report of the destruction of their (Yādava) friends from Arjuna, your grandfathers installed you (on the throne of Hastināpura) as the successor of their dynasty, and all of them proceeded to the Great path (to the other world).

devanagari: य एतद् देवदेवस्य विष्णोः कर्माणि जन्म च । कीर्तयेत् श्रद्धया मर्त्यः सर्वपापैः प्रमुच्यते ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: ya etad devadevasya viṣṇoḥ karmāṇi janma ca kīrtayet śraddhayā martyaḥ sarvapāpaiḥ pramucyate
translation: The moral (person) who with devout faith would glorify the incarnations and sportive acts of Lord Viṣṇu (Kṛṣṇa), the God of gods becomes absolved of all sins.

devanagari: इत्थं हरेर्भगवतो रुचिरावतार । वीर्याणि बालचरितानि च शंतमानि । अन्यत्र चेह च श्रुतानि गृणन् मनुष्यो । भक्तिं परां परमहंसगतौ लभेत ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: itthaṃ harerbhagavato rucirāvatāra vīryāṇi bālacaritāni ca śaṃtamāni anyatra ceha ca śrutāni gṛṇan manuṣyo bhaktiṃ parāṃ paramahaṃsagatau labheta

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

[nominative plural from √ śru class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ śru class 5 verb], [accusative plural from √ śru class 5 verb]
[optative active second plural], [optative middle third single]

translation: He who sings the above-mentioned auspicious-most sportive acts of the glorious Lord Hari in his boyhood, and his delightful incarnation and great feats achieved therein-which he has heard here (in the Bhāgavata Purāṇa or elsewhere, would have engendered in him, supreme devotion and would attain to the goal reached only by Parama Haṃsas (i.e. recluses of the highest order).

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां एकादशस्कन्धे एकत्रिंशोऽध्यायः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ ekādaśaskandhe ekatriṃśo
